revision.text:
{{Infobox Wikipedia user script |name=DiffUndo |desc=<onlyinclude>Adds an undo button to each line on the diff while editing</onlyinclude> |browsers=Chrome, Firefox, etc. ([[ES2020]]+ needed) |source=[[User:Nardog/DiffUndo.js]] }}
This script adds an undo button to each line on the diff between the current version of the page and yours while editing ("Show changes"). Though inspired by a similar feature in [[Wikipedia:AutoWikiBrowser|AutoWikiBrowser]], it also allows you to [[redo]] an undone change by clicking the button again. The button is visible only while the cursor or focus is inside a line.
It comes in handy especially when reverting edits. If you want to revert only part of an edit, you can click "undo" on page history or a diff and keep certain changes by undoing on the diff. If you want to revert an old edit but MediaWiki's native undo feature doesn't work because later edits interfere, you can go to the editing page of the previous revision, click "Show changes", and restore all the non-problematic edits by undoing the lines.
==Installation== Add the following to [[Special:MyPage/common.js|your common.js]] or [[meta:Special:MyPage/global.js|global.js]]: <syntaxhighlight lang="javascript"> mw.loader.load('//en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=User:Nardog/DiffUndo.js&action=raw&ctype=text/javascript'); </syntaxhighlight>
Or you may enable the [[User:Enterprisey/script-installer|script installer]] in {{myprefs|Gadgets}} and click "Install" in the infobox on this page.
==How it works== You can undo a change as long as the line in the current source (deduced from the closest line number on the diff) matches the line in the "Your text" column of the diff. In case of a removed line (i.e. the cell in the "Your text" column is empty), the preceding line is compared. A redo works just like that except the columns are reversed.
==Limitations== *Undoing a change may clear the browser's native undo cache ({{key press|Ctrl|Z}}). *VisualEditor and the [[mw:2017 wikitext editor|2017 wikitext editor]] are not supported.
{{User:Nardog/scripts}}

{{Infobox Wikipedia user script |name=DiffUndo |desc=<onlyinclude>Adds an undo button to each line on the diff while editing</onlyinclude> |browsers=Chrome, Firefox, etc. ([[ES2020]]+ needed) |source=[[User:Nardog/DiffUndo.js]] }}
This script adds an undo button to each line on the diff between the current version of the page and yours while editing ("Show changes"). Though inspired by a similar feature in [[Wikipedia:AutoWikiBrowser|AutoWikiBrowser]], it also allows you to [[redo]] an undone change by clicking the button again. The button is visible only while the cursor or focus is inside a line.
It comes in handy especially when reverting edits. If you want to revert only part of an edit, you can click "undo" on page history or a diff and keep certain changes by undoing on the diff. If you want to revert an old edit but MediaWiki's native undo feature doesn't work because later edits interfere, you can go to the editing page of the previous revision, click "Show changes", and restore all the non-problematic edits by undoing the lines.
==Installation== Add the following to [[Special:MyPage/common.js|your common.js]] or [[meta:Special:MyPage/global.js|global.js]]: <syntaxhighlight lang="javascript"> mw.loader.load('//en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=User:Nardog/DiffUndo.js&action=raw&ctype=text/javascript'); </syntaxhighlight>
Or you may enable the [[User:Enterprisey/script-installer|script installer]] in {{myprefs|Gadgets}} and click "Install" in the infobox on this page.
==How it works== You can undo a change as long as the line in the current source (deduced from the closest line number on the diff) matches the line in the "Your text" column of the diff. In case of a removed line (i.e. the cell in the "Your text" column is empty), the preceding line is compared. A redo works just like that except the columns are reversed.
==Limitations== *Undoing a change may clear the browser's native undo cache ({{key press|Ctrl|Z}}). *VisualEditor and the [[mw:2017 wikitext editor|2017 wikitext editor]] are not supported.
{{User:Nardog/scripts}}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Infobox Wikipedia user script |name=DiffUndo |desc=<onlyinclude>Adds an undo button to each line on the diff while editing</onlyinclude> |browsers=Chrome, Firefox, etc. ([[:en:ES2020|ES2020]]+ needed) |source=[[User:Nardog/DiffUndo.js]] }}
This script adds an undo button to each line on the diff between the current version of the page and yours while editing ("Show changes"). Though inspired by a similar feature in [[Wikipedia:AutoWikiBrowser|AutoWikiBrowser]], it also allows you to [[redo]] an undone change by clicking the button again. The button is visible only while the cursor or focus is inside a line.
It comes in handy especially when reverting edits. If you want to revert only part of an edit, you can click "undo" on page history or a diff and keep certain changes by undoing on the diff. If you want to revert an old edit but MediaWiki's native undo feature doesn't work because later edits interfere, you can go to the editing page of the previous revision, click "Show changes", and restore all the non-problematic edits by undoing the lines.
==Installation== Add the following to [[Special:MyPage/common.js|your common.js]] or [[meta:Special:MyPage/global.js|global.js]]: <syntaxhighlight lang="javascript"> mw.loader.load('//en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=User:Nardog/DiffUndo.js&action=raw&ctype=text/javascript'); </syntaxhighlight>
Or you may enable the [[User:Enterprisey/script-installer|script installer]] in {{myprefs|Gadgets}} and click "Install" in the infobox on this page.
==How it works== You can undo a change as long as the line in the current source (deduced from the closest line number on the diff) matches the line in the "Your text" column of the diff. In case of a removed line (i.e. the cell in the "Your text" column is empty), the preceding line is compared. A redo works just like that except the columns are reversed.
==Limitations== *Undoing a change may clear the browser's native undo cache ({{key press|Ctrl|Z}}). *VisualEditor and the [[mw:2017 wikitext editor|2017 wikitext editor]] are not supported.
{{User:Nardog/scripts}}

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Infobox Wikipedia user script |name=DiffUndo |desc=<onlyinclude>Adds an undo button to each line on the diff while editing</onlyinclude> |browsers=Chrome, Firefox, etc. ([[:en:ES2020|ES2020]]+ needed) |source=[[User:Nardog/DiffUndo.js]] }}
This script adds an undo button to each line on the diff between the current version of the page and yours while editing ("Show changes"). Though inspired by a similar feature in [[Wikipedia:AutoWikiBrowser|AutoWikiBrowser]], it also allows you to [[:en:Redo|Redo]] an undone change by clicking the button again. The button is visible only while the cursor or focus is inside a line.
It comes in handy especially when reverting edits. If you want to revert only part of an edit, you can click "undo" on page history or a diff and keep certain changes by undoing on the diff. If you want to revert an old edit but MediaWiki's native undo feature doesn't work because later edits interfere, you can go to the editing page of the previous revision, click "Show changes", and restore all the non-problematic edits by undoing the lines.
==Installation== Add the following to [[Special:MyPage/common.js|your common.js]] or [[meta:Special:MyPage/global.js|global.js]]: <syntaxhighlight lang="javascript"> mw.loader.load('//en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=User:Nardog/DiffUndo.js&action=raw&ctype=text/javascript'); </syntaxhighlight>
Or you may enable the [[User:Enterprisey/script-installer|script installer]] in {{myprefs|Gadgets}} and click "Install" in the infobox on this page.
==How it works== You can undo a change as long as the line in the current source (deduced from the closest line number on the diff) matches the line in the "Your text" column of the diff. In case of a removed line (i.e. the cell in the "Your text" column is empty), the preceding line is compared. A redo works just like that except the columns are reversed.
==Limitations== *Undoing a change may clear the browser's native undo cache ({{key press|Ctrl|Z}}). *VisualEditor and the [[mw:2017 wikitext editor|2017 wikitext editor]] are not supported.
{{User:Nardog/scripts}}

{{Infobox Wikipedia user script |name=DiffUndo |desc=<onlyinclude>Adds an undo button to each line on the diff while editing</onlyinclude> |browsers=Chrome, Firefox, etc. ([[:en:ES2020|ES2020]]+ needed) |source=[[User:Nardog/DiffUndo.js]] }}
This script adds an undo button to each line on the diff between the current version of the page and yours while editing ("Show changes"). Though inspired by a similar feature in [[Wikipedia:AutoWikiBrowser|AutoWikiBrowser]], it also allows you to [[:en:Redo|Redo]] an undone change by clicking the button again. The button is visible only while the cursor or focus is inside a line.
It comes in handy especially when reverting edits. If you want to revert only part of an edit, you can click "undo" on page history or a diff and keep certain changes by undoing on the diff. If you want to revert an old edit but MediaWiki's native undo feature doesn't work because later edits interfere, you can go to the editing page of the previous revision, click "Show changes", and restore all the non-problematic edits by undoing the lines.
==Installation== Add the following to [[Special:MyPage/common.js|your common.js]] or [[meta:Special:MyPage/global.js|global.js]]: <syntaxhighlight lang="javascript"> mw.loader.load('//en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=User:Nardog/DiffUndo.js&action=raw&ctype=text/javascript'); </syntaxhighlight>
Or you may enable the [[User:Enterprisey/script-installer|script installer]] in {{myprefs|Gadgets}} and click "Install" in the infobox on this page.
==How it works== You can undo a change as long as the line in the current source (deduced from the closest line number on the diff) matches the line in the "Your text" column of the diff. In case of a removed line (i.e. the cell in the "Your text" column is empty), the preceding line is compared. A redo works just like that except the columns are reversed.
==Limitations== *Undoing a change may clear the browser's native undo cache ({{key press|Ctrl|Z}}). *VisualEditor and the [[mw:2017 wikitext editor|2017 wikitext editor]] are not supported.
{{User:Nardog/scripts}}[[Category:reimport]]

{{Infobox Wikipedia user script |name=DiffUndo |desc=<onlyinclude>Adds an undo button to each line on the diff while editing</onlyinclude> |browsers=Chrome, Firefox, etc. ([[ES2020]]+ needed) |source=[[User:Nardog/DiffUndo.js]] }}
This script adds an undo button to each line on the diff between the current version of the page and yours while editing ("Show changes"). Though inspired by a similar feature in [[Wikipedia:AutoWikiBrowser|AutoWikiBrowser]], it also allows you to [[redo]] an undone change by clicking the button again. The button is visible only while the cursor or focus is inside a line.
It comes in handy especially when reverting edits. If you want to revert only part of an edit, you can click "undo" on page history or a diff and keep certain changes by undoing on the diff. If you want to revert an old edit but MediaWiki's native undo feature doesn't work because later edits interfere, you can go to the editing page of the previous revision, click "Show changes", and restore all the non-problematic edits by undoing the lines.
==Installation== Add the following to [[Special:MyPage/common.js|your common.js]] or [[meta:Special:MyPage/global.js|global.js]]: <syntaxhighlight lang="javascript"> mw.loader.load('//en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=User:Nardog/DiffUndo.js&action=raw&ctype=text/javascript'); </syntaxhighlight>
Or you may enable the [[User:Enterprisey/script-installer|script installer]] in {{myprefs|Gadgets}} and click "Install" in the infobox on this page.
==How it works== You can undo a change as long as the line in the current source (deduced from the closest line number on the diff) matches the line in the "Your text" column of the diff. In case of a removed line (i.e. the cell in the "Your text" column is empty), the preceding line is compared. A redo works just like that except the columns are reversed.
==Limitations== *Lines involving [[mw:Help:Pre-save transform|pre-save transforms]], such as [[Help:Substitution|{{tlc|subst:}}]] and signatures, cannot be undone. *Undoing a change may clear the browser's native undo cache ({{key press|Ctrl|Z}}). *VisualEditor and the [[mw:2017 wikitext editor|2017 wikitext editor]] are not supported.
{{User:Nardog/scripts}}

{{Infobox Wikipedia user script |name=DiffUndo |desc=<onlyinclude>Adds an undo button to each line on the diff while editing</onlyinclude> |browsers=Chrome, Firefox, etc. ([[ES2020]]+ needed) |source=[[User:Nardog/DiffUndo.js]] }}
This script adds an undo button to each line on the diff between the current version of the page and yours while editing ("Show changes"). Though inspired by a similar feature in [[Wikipedia:AutoWikiBrowser|AutoWikiBrowser]], it also allows you to [[redo]] an undone change by clicking the button again. The button is visible only while the cursor or focus is inside a line.
It comes in handy especially when reverting edits. If you want to revert only part of an edit, you can click "undo" on page history or a diff and keep certain changes by undoing on the diff. If you want to revert an old edit but MediaWiki's native undo feature doesn't work because later edits interfere, you can go to the editing page of the previous revision, click "Show changes", and restore all the non-problematic edits by undoing the lines.
==Installation== Add the following to [[Special:MyPage/common.js|your common.js]] or [[meta:Special:MyPage/global.js|global.js]]: <syntaxhighlight lang="javascript"> mw.loader.load('//en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=User:Nardog/DiffUndo.js&action=raw&ctype=text/javascript'); </syntaxhighlight>
Or you may enable the [[User:Enterprisey/script-installer|script installer]] in {{myprefs|Gadgets}} and click "Install" in the infobox on this page.
==How it works== You can undo a change as long as the line in the current source (deduced from the closest line number on the diff) matches the line in the "Your text" column of the diff. In case of a removed line (i.e. the cell in the "Your text" column is empty), the preceding line is compared. A redo works just like that except the columns are reversed.
==Limitations== *Lines involving [[mw:Help:Pre-save transform|pre-save transforms]], such as [[Help:Substitution|{{tlc|subst:}}]] and signatures, cannot be undone. *Undoing a change may clear the browser's native undo cache ({{key press|Ctrl|Z}}). *VisualEditor and the [[mw:2017 wikitext editor|2017 wikitext editor]] are not supported.
{{User:Nardog/scripts}}

revision.comment: [[Project:History cleanup|Offloading history due to miraheze stability concerns]] many contributors, see [https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=Nardog%2FDiffUndo here] for full history [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24796114356|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!-- History offloaded: due to miraheze stability concerns, the edit history of this page has been offloaded to the XML archive. See https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=Nardog%2FDiffUndo for full history. Last refreshed: [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24796114356|commit triggered pipeline]] --> {{Infobox Wikipedia user script |name=DiffUndo |desc=<onlyinclude>Adds an undo button to each line on the diff while editing</onlyinclude> |browsers=Chrome, Firefox, etc. ([[ES2020]]+ needed) |source=[[User:Nardog/DiffUndo.js]] }}
This script adds an undo button to each line on the diff between the current version of the page and yours while editing ("Show changes"). Though inspired by a similar feature in [[Wikipedia:AutoWikiBrowser|AutoWikiBrowser]], it also allows you to [[redo]] an undone change by clicking the button again. The button is visible only while the cursor or focus is inside a line.
It comes in handy especially when reverting edits. If you want to revert only part of an edit, you can click "undo" on page history or a diff and keep certain changes by undoing on the diff. If you want to revert an old edit but MediaWiki's native undo feature doesn't work because later edits interfere, you can go to the editing page of the previous revision, click "Show changes", and restore all the non-problematic edits by undoing the lines.
==Installation== Add the following to [[Special:MyPage/common.js|your common.js]] or [[meta:Special:MyPage/global.js|global.js]]: <syntaxhighlight lang="javascript"> mw.loader.load('//en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=User:Nardog/DiffUndo.js&action=raw&ctype=text/javascript'); </syntaxhighlight>
Or you may enable the [[User:Enterprisey/script-installer|script installer]] in {{myprefs|Gadgets}} and click "Install" in the infobox on this page.
==How it works== You can undo a change as long as the line in the current source (deduced from the closest line number on the diff) matches the line in the "Your text" column of the diff. In case of a removed line (i.e. the cell in the "Your text" column is empty), the preceding line is compared. A redo works just like that except the columns are reversed.
==Limitations== *Lines involving [[mw:Help:Pre-save transform|pre-save transforms]], such as [[Help:Substitution|{{tlc|subst:}}]] and signatures, cannot be undone. *Undoing a change may clear the browser's native undo cache ({{key press|Ctrl|Z}}). *VisualEditor and the [[mw:2017 wikitext editor|2017 wikitext editor]] are not supported.
{{User:Nardog/scripts}}
